title: Bootstrap service-registry.json for terminalbeta
description: service-skills-sync specialist and scope.py require a service-registry.json at .claude/skills/service-registry.json. This repo has none. Steps: (1) run /creating-service-skills to scaffold the registry; (2) update xt-init + sp init to include service-registry creation as standard setup step.

title: get_economic_events country filter returns 0 for 'united states' despite events present
description:
After the calendar DB was populated (fix on 2026-03-17), the unfiltered call returns US events correctly. But filtering by countries=['united states'] returns 0 events.

Verified:
  get_economic_events(time_range='this_week')
  -> 200+ events including rows with country='united states' (NY Empire State, Industrial Production, ADP, Pending Home Sales, Trump Speaks, FOMC-week events)

  get_economic_events(time_range='this_week', countries=['united states'])
  -> event[0]{}:  <- empty

  get_economic_events(time_range='this_week', countries=['united states'], importance=['high','medium'])
  -> event[0]{}:  <- empty

The string 'united states' IS stored in the DB (confirmed from unfiltered output). Tool docs say 'Names MUST be lowercase' — they are lowercase. This is a server-side filtering bug, not a caller error.

FOMC week: this makes the tool unusable for the most important US macro events of the week.

title: get_options_data all 4 categories returning 7-week stale reports
description:
All four get_options_data categories return reports from late January 2026 — approximately 7 weeks stale as of 2026-03-17:
- EQUITY: 2026-01-30
- TREASURY: 2026-01-30
- SOFR: 2026-01-29
- ENERGY: 2026-01-29

Tool documentation says 'Data available only for business days (daily reports)'. Expected latest date: 2026-03-14 (last Friday).

Impact: CME daily PDF options reports (Volume, OI, Skew) not being parsed and stored. Key level analysis, flow observations, and sentiment signals are 7 weeks stale. During FOMC week this is particularly significant.

Needs: check CME PDF downloader/parser service status.

title: get_economic_events returns 0 events for all time ranges — calendar DB empty
description:
get_economic_events returns 0 events for every time_range and country combination tested, including:
- time_range=today, this_week, yesterday, last_week
- countries=[united states], [united states, united kingdom, european union]
- importance=[high], all

This is FOMC week (March 17-19, 2026) — there should be dozens of high-importance US events.

Confirmed NOT a timing gap: last_week US high importance also returns 0, so the DB appears empty or queries are broken.

Previous incident (2026-03-16): data was absent at 18:51 UTC but appeared at 19:07 UTC — that was a timing gap. This is different — persists across all time ranges including historical.

Needs: check econ_calendar_updater container status and DB row counts in the events table.
close_reason: Fixed 2026-03-17. Calendar DB populated. New related bugs filed as terminalbeta-* (country filter returning 0 for 'united states' despite data present, and mass mis-tagging of non-EU events as 'european union').

title: Session not found after container restart — pubfinance MCP
description:
Same as market-data session issue. Restarting pubfinance-mcp container invalidates all active MCP sessions. Clients get 'Session not found' on all 19 pubfinance tools until they restart their client.

Reproduced on 2026-03-16 when pubfinance-mcp was restarted to deploy the get_data_freshness fix. Full session loss confirmed — required Claude Code restart to recover.

Fix: same options as market-data session issue — stateless auth preferred for zero-downtime deployments.

title: Session not found after container restart — market-data MCP
description:
Restarting the mmd-mcp-server Docker container invalidates all active MCP sessions. Clients receive 'Session not found' on every subsequent tool call and must restart their entire client (e.g. Claude Code) to re-establish the session. This happened during a fix deployment on 2026-03-16 and rendered all 18 market-data tools inaccessible until client restart.

Root cause: HTTP MCP server stores session state in-process; container restart drops all session IDs. Client has no automatic reconnect.

Fix options:
1. Implement stateless auth (API key in header, no server-side session)
2. Session migration: persist session IDs to Redis/DB, survive restarts
3. Expose a /health endpoint clients can use to detect restart and re-init

Affected: all tools on mercury-market-data (18 tools)

title: Restructure Mercury user guides into website-ready documentation IA
description:
PROBLEM: Current Mercury userguides in /home/dawid/second-mind/1-projects/mercury/userguides are source-backed and accurate, but still read like internal engineering/reference notes. They need to be reorganized into a professional public documentation structure suitable for the Mercury website, closer to Atlassian/Stripe-style docs while preserving Mercury's institutional trading voice.

SUCCESS: Userguides become a navigable public-docs information architecture with clear landing page, get-started docs, task-oriented workflow pages, split tool references, concept pages, troubleshooting, and internal verification material separated from public docs. using-mercury-session-guide is synced with the new canonical using-mercury workflow hub. Newsletter/Darth Feedor workflows are promoted as a first-class differentiator.

SCOPE:
- Target output folder: /home/dawid/second-mind/1-projects/mercury/userguides
- Create/adjust public docs IA:
  1. README.md as docs landing page
  2. get-started/ docs: what-is-mercury, install-claude-desktop, install-claude-code, first-session
  3. workflows/ docs: morning-brief, market-scan, instrument-deep-dive, rates-stir, liquidity-pubfinance, newsletter-research
  4. tools/ split reference: market-data, darth-feedor, econ-data, pubfinance
  5. concepts/ explainers: AMT, vol regime, LCI/liquidity pillars, curve/STIR, newsletter retrieval model
  6. troubleshooting/ docs: MCP connection, API key, Desktop extension, stale/missing data
  7. internal/ or verification/ section for source-map and live MCP verification
- Add a docs style guide defining page template, tone, callouts, examples, and naming.
- Preserve source-backed claims and live MCP/tool names already verified.
- Keep obsolete get_options_data out of public docs except if mentioned only as resolved historical drift.

NON_GOALS:
- Do not change Mercury MCP source code.
- Do not expose API keys or secrets.
- Do not rewrite terminalbeta installer behavior in this issue.
- Do not attempt full website integration/deployment; only vault markdown docs.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Follow public docs style: task-oriented, concise, professional, outcome-first.
- Keep Mercury voice: institutional desk analyst, not generic SaaS marketing.
- For tool details, prefer concise user-facing summaries over raw source docstring dumps; keep raw verification in internal docs.
- Maintain Obsidian-compatible wikilinks where useful.
- After docs edits, run vaultctl index --source vault --json and verify search finds new docs.

VALIDATION:
- File tree exists and matches intended IA.
- README links to major sections.
- Each workflow page has: purpose, before you begin, steps, example prompt, what good output looks like, related tools.
- Tools reference is split by MCP service and includes current live tool names/parameters at user-friendly depth.
- Newsletter workflow page covers list_articles -> get_article_bullets -> article_graph/progressive_article_search/search_article_bullets/aggregate_articles and explains recency vs relevance.
- vaultctl index succeeds and a search for 'newsletter research Mercury' returns the new docs.

OUTPUT:
- Updated markdown documentation tree under userguides.
- Brief summary of created/changed files and any follow-up issues discovered.
notes: Correction after final reconciliation: terminalbeta/docs is the website package and contains only the 6 publish:true user-facing docs with 0 broken links. The user-maintained vault copy is /home/dawid/second-mind/1-projects/Mercury/userguides; fixed moved-page stub archive links there and rechecked: 40 md, 6 publish:true, 26 publish:false, 0 broken links.

title: Draft Mercury MCP user guides in Obsidian vault
description: PROBLEM: Mercury needs precise user-facing markdown documentation in /home/dawid/second-mind/1-projects/mercury/userguides covering installation, using-mercury startup skill, user workflow skills, and every MCP tool across market-data, darth-feedor, economic-data, and treasury-cb/pubfinance. SUCCESS: source-backed docs are produced with clear install/config guidance and tool-by-tool usage/reference. SCOPE: terminalbeta installer/skills plus source repos market-data, darth-feedor, economic-data, treasury-cb; output folder in second-mind vault. NON_GOALS: no production code changes, no secret exposure, no destructive operations. CONSTRAINTS: verify terminalbeta mcp installer and source docstrings; if live MCP access is unavailable, document from source and note setup needed. VALIDATION: docs cite source paths and are internally consistent. OUTPUT: markdown docs and summary.
notes: Improved using-mercury as canonical Desktop/minimal workflow hub: rewritten SKILL.md with current tool truths, workflow router, default sequencing, automatic escalations, detailed workflow recipes, newsletter research flow, compact tool reference, and closing pattern. Rebuilt using-mercury.skill with references bundled. Validated no invalid allowed-tools and no stale get_options_data string in package.

title: Mass country mis-tagging — non-EU events incorrectly labelled 'european union'
description:
A large proportion of events in the calendar DB are tagged with country='european union' when they clearly belong to other countries. Observed in this_week unfiltered results:

Examples of mis-tagged events:
  european union | WPI Inflation (YoY) (Feb)         <- India (INR currency, WPI is India-specific)
  european union | Chinese Unemployment Rate (Feb)   <- China
  european union | Fixed Asset Investment (Feb)       <- China
  european union | Brazilian IPCA Inflation Index     <- Brazil
  european union | Trade Balance (Feb) [NOK]          <- Norway
  european union | Budget balance (Feb) [TRY]         <- Turkey
  european union | RBNZ Offshore Holdings             <- New Zealand
  european union | RBA Interest Rate Decision (Mar)   <- Australia (RBA = Reserve Bank of Australia)
  european union | German ZEW Economic Sentiment      <- Germany (correctly EU but should be 'germany')
  european union | 5-Year Treasury Gilt Auction       <- United Kingdom (also tagged as 'united kingdom' separately — duplicate)

Impact:
1. Filtering by country='india', 'brazil', 'australia', 'new zealand', 'norway', 'turkey' will return 0 results
2. Filtering by country='european union' returns an enormous noisy set mixing EU events with global events
3. Many events appear duplicated (once as 'european union', once with correct country)

Root cause: likely the ingestion pipeline assigns a default country of 'european union' when the source country cannot be parsed, rather than leaving it null or using an 'unknown' value.

title: get_correlation_matrix returns only 5 hardcoded pairs instead of full universe
description:
get_correlation_matrix returns 5 pairs only:
  CL=F|ES=F, ES=F|NQ=F, ZN=F|6J=F, ZN=F|ES=F, ZN=F|GC=F

Tool description says 'Multi-timeframe correlation between all tracked instruments' (19 instruments = 171 possible pairs).

These 5 appear to be statically defined, not dynamically computed. Missing important relationships: ES/GC (risk-off hedge), ES/RTY (breadth), 6B or 6S pairs, ZB/ZN (duration), ZN/ZF (curve), commodity crosses.

The switch_risk_pct=96.1 on CL/ES is a significant signal (extreme instability) — but this flag is invisible for the 166 other pairs not returned.

Request: either return all pairs or add symbol filtering parameter (e.g. symbols=['ES=F','ZN=F']) so callers can query specific relationships.

title: reference_rates missing 2 business days — Thu/Fri ingestion lag
description:
get_reference_rates latest record is 2026-03-12 (Wednesday). Missing: Thu 2026-03-13 and Fri 2026-03-14. As of Monday 2026-03-17 this is a 3-business-day lag.

NY Fed publishes SOFR/EFFR/BGCR/TGCR/OBFR the following business morning. Thu rates should have been available by Fri morning, Fri rates by Mon morning.

get_reference_rates(days=14):
  2026-03-12|3.6500|3.6300|3.6300|3.6400|3.6400  <- most recent
  (2026-03-13 missing)
  (2026-03-14 missing)

During FOMC week this data is especially critical. Reference rates are the foundation of the LCI Monetary Index.

title: TGA_Balance and VIX/credit spreads null in fed_liquidity
description:
TGA_Balance is null across all 7 days of get_fed_liquidity history. VIX, HY_OAS, BBB_OAS are also null in get_fed_liquidity_latest.

Verified: get_fiscal_daily_latest returns TGA_Balance=806335 (2026-03-13) — data exists in fiscal tables but is not synced to fed_liquidity_daily.

Consequence: Net_Liquidity is calculated as Fed_Total_Assets - RRP - TGA. With TGA null (treated as 0), Net_Liquidity reads as 6,645,762 — overstated by ~B compared to the true value.

get_fed_liquidity_latest response shows:
  TGA_Balance|Net_Liquidity
  (empty)    |6645762.0000

True net liquidity should be approximately 6645762 - 806 ≈ 5,839,762 (billions).

title: ret_5d_pct empty in all bundle instrument responses
description:
ret_5d_pct is consistently null in the instruments section across all four bundle endpoints. Only ret_1d_pct is populated.

Affected: get_equities_bundle, get_fx_bundle, get_commodities_bundle, get_treasuries_bundle, get_stir_bundle.

Example:
  get_equities_bundle()
  instruments[4]{symbol|last_price|session_state|ret_1d_pct|ret_5d_pct|vol_regime}:
    ES=F|6803.25|ETH|0.0076||Spike-Rising  <- ret_5d_pct empty

The field IS populated in:
- get_market_overview: ES=F|6803.25|RTH|0.0076|0.0009|...
- get_symbol_detail: returns_profile.ret_5d=0.0009

The data exists — the bundle assembler is not mapping it.
notes:
Enhancement request (2026-03-17): while fixing ret_5d_pct, also add an optional 'days' parameter to all bundle and overview endpoints so callers can choose the lookback for both return and range-position fields:

- ret_Nd_pct: e.g. days=20 returns ret_20d_pct instead of (or alongside) ret_5d_pct
- pos_Nd: e.g. days=20 returns pos_20d instead of the fixed pos_1d/pos_5d/pos_20d trio

This avoids hardcoding the 1d/5d/20d trio and lets callers tailor the lookback to their timeframe (e.g. days=60 for positional context over the quarter). Affects: get_equities_bundle, get_fx_bundle, get_commodities_bundle, get_treasuries_bundle, get_stir_bundle, get_market_overview.

title: get_economic_history tool missing — 59 series in catalog with no retrieval endpoint
description:
list_economic_history_catalog returns 59 series metadata (CPI, NFP, GDP, PCE, ECI, PPI, JOLTS, etc.) with indicator_name, calc_type, source, category, first_date, last_date, data_points. But there is no get_economic_history tool to retrieve the actual data.

The catalog is discoverable but the data is inaccessible via MCP.

Example series available but not retrievable:
  CPI (YoY, NSA)|yoy|BLS|inflation|2007-01-01|2026-02-01|229
  Nonfarm Payrolls (SA)|level|BLS|employment|2007-01-01|2026-02-01|230
  Core CPI (YoY, NSA)|yoy|BLS|inflation|2007-01-01|2026-02-01|229

Request: add get_economic_history(indicator_name, start_date, end_date) or get_economic_history(series_id) tool. This would enable historical charting, surprise analysis (actual vs forecast over time), and trend context.

title: No rate limit headers or documentation — parallel calls trigger silent 429s
description:
All Mercury MCP servers return HTTP 429 when parallel request volume is high, but:
1. No rate limit is documented anywhere
2. No Retry-After or X-RateLimit headers are returned with 429 responses
3. Callers have no way to know they are approaching a limit before being rejected

Observed: sending ~65 parallel calls across all 4 servers triggered 429s on darth-feedor and market-data. The threshold is unknown.

Impact: automated workflows (morning briefs, session scans) that call multiple endpoints in parallel will silently fail with no backoff signal.

Fix: add X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After headers to all 429 responses. Document per-server rate limits in tool descriptions.

title: data_source_status table orphaned — pipeline no longer writes to it
description:
The data_source_status table in treasury-cb is not updated by the current pipeline. It last had records written in December 2025. This caused get_data_freshness to report stale Dec 2025 timestamps for all sources — even though the underlying data was current.

Fixed in 2026-03-16: get_data_freshness rewritten to compute freshness directly from actual data tables (fed_liquidity_daily, fiscal_daily_metrics, nyfed_repo_ops).

However the root cause remains: data_source_status is never written to. Either:
1. Remove the table entirely and update any remaining references
2. Wire the pipeline back to call BaseIngestor.update_metadata() after each ingest run

The table is dead code masquerading as a live data source.

title: get_research stale — all docs from Oct 2025, no recent ingestion
description:
get_research returns 5 PDF documents all dated October 2025 — 5+ months stale as of March 2026. Research ingestion pipeline appears stopped.

get_research(has_summary=True, limit=5) returns docs from JPM, ANZ, and other publishers with doc_date in Oct 2025 only. No newer documents in DB.

Impact: the research tool is effectively unusable for current market context. Research reports from late 2025 through March 2026 are missing entirely.

title: relevance_score null on all recent articles (IDs 7170+)
description:
get_articles returns relevance_score=null for all articles with IDs 7170 and above (recent articles). The field is present in the schema but not populated.

Confirmed: get_article_detail on older article ID 7167 returns relevance_score=82. The scoring pipeline runs as a separate step after LLM summarisation and appears to have stopped for recent ingestions.

Example:
  get_articles(source='gmail-substack', has_summary=True, limit=5)
  7174|...|2026-03-17 13:40:55|  <- relevance_score empty
  7173|...|2026-03-17 13:09:00|  <- relevance_score empty

relevance_score is used for ranking in get_articles and filtering by quality. Null values degrade search relevance.

title: get_lci history rows missing inline regime label
description:
get_lci(days=N) returns raw LCI values but LCI_Regime is not reliably present in history rows. Callers must apply the threshold mapping manually or call get_lci_summary separately for the interpretation.

Threshold mapping:
  LCI > +1.5  → Very Loose
  LCI +0.5 to +1.5 → Loose
  LCI -0.5 to +0.5 → Neutral
  LCI -1.5 to -0.5 → Tight
  LCI < -1.5 → Very Tight

Observed in get_lci(days=7):
  2026-03-16|0.4489|...|Neutral  <- LCI_Regime present (sometimes)
  2026-03-15|0.5094|...|Easy     <- 'Easy' is not in the documented regime scale

The LCI_Regime field is inconsistent — 'Easy' appears where 'Loose' is expected per docs. Either the thresholds changed and docs are stale, or the field is being populated differently in history vs summary.

Request: ensure LCI_Regime is computed consistently as a CASE expression in the query and matches the documented scale.

title: tool_health_check missing on market-data, pubfinance, and econ-data
description:
Only mercury-darth-feedor exposes a tool_health_check endpoint. The other three servers (market-data, pubfinance, econ-data) have no equivalent.

darth-feedor health_check returns: server status, DB connection, uptime. Essential for quick liveness verification before running a full workflow.

Without health checks on the other servers, the only way to verify they are alive is to call a data tool and check for a non-error response — which conflates connectivity with data availability.

Request: add tool_health_check (or health_check) to mercury-market-data, mercury-pubfinance, and mercury-econ-data returning at minimum: status, db_connected, uptime/version.

title: get_candles no symbol discovery — callers must guess valid symbols
description:
get_candles requires a symbol parameter but provides no discovery mechanism. Callers must already know valid symbols (ES=F, ZN=F, etc.) or infer them from get_market_overview results.

If a caller passes an invalid symbol, they get an empty result or error with no helpful message listing valid symbols.

Request: add a get_candles_symbols or reuse get_market_overview symbol list as the canonical source. At minimum, document the complete list of valid symbols for get_candles in the tool description, and clarify whether spread symbols (TUT, EF-M26) are supported.

title: get_options_data does not support toon format — inconsistent with all other tools
description:
get_options_data returns raw JSON regardless of any format parameter. All other market-data tools support toon format (compact pipe-delimited, 50-60% token reduction).

The options response is a nested JSON blob with summary, sentiment, key_levels, and significant_flows. A toon representation would need a flat schema — possible approach:
  options_report{date|type|sentiment|summary}:
    2026-03-14|EQUITY_OPTIONS|NEUTRAL|<truncated prose>
  key_levels[N]{level}: ...
  flows[N]{asset|observation}: ...

Alternatively: keep JSON but document it explicitly as JSON-only in the tool description to set caller expectations.

title: get_correlation_matrix missing regime_unstable boolean flag
description:
get_correlation_matrix returns switch_risk_pct (0-100%) indicating regime transition probability. The threshold for instability is documented as >30% = unstable. But callers must implement this threshold themselves.

Observed: CL/ES has switch_risk_pct=96.1 — extremely unstable correlation regime. This is an important signal that could be missed.

Request: add a computed boolean field regime_unstable (switch_risk_pct > 30) alongside switch_risk_pct. This mirrors the pattern used in get_volatility_metrics where thresholds are pre-computed (vol_switch_risk >30% = unstable).

title: aggregate_articles missing tags filter — add tags and tag_match params
description:
aggregate_articles supports filtering by category (Macro/Policy/Equities/Geopolitics/Noise) and source only. list_articles supports tags and tag_match filters. aggregate_articles does not.

Use case: 'Synthesise all articles from the last 3 days tagged #fed or #fomc' — currently not possible. category=Policy is the closest but too broad.

Request: add tags: list[str] | None and tag_match: str = 'any' parameters matching the same semantics as list_articles.

title: topic:<name> view names undocumented in get_squawk_context
description:
get_squawk_context supports view='topic:<name>' to filter events_today by domain. The tool description gives examples ('topic:Geopolitics', 'topic:Central Banks') but does not list all valid topic names.

Callers must guess or discover valid names by trial and error. Invalid topic names silently return empty results.

Request: add a list of valid topic names to the tool description. Alternatively, add a view='topics' that returns the available topic names for the current session.

title: get_article_bullets identical to list_articles stubs — differentiate or remove
description:
get_article_bullets(ids=[...]) returns the exact same fields as list_articles stubs: id, title, source, published_at, category, thesis, bullets, tags. There is no additional value from calling it over list_articles.

Tool description says it is 'phase 2 of two-phase retrieval' — intended to fetch bullet points for selected IDs after list_articles discovery. But the bullets field is already included in list_articles stubs.

Options:
1. Differentiate: return expanded bullet text (full prose, not comma-joined), or add fields like sentiment, mechanisms that are not in stubs
2. Remove: consolidate into list_articles with an ids filter parameter
3. Keep as convenience: document explicitly that it's a filtered list_articles by IDs (useful for retrieving specific IDs without re-running a search)

title: get_squawk_context history mode only returns same-day synthesis runs
description:
get_squawk_context(mode='history', limit=5) returns multiple rows but all are from the same calendar day (today). Each row represents a synthesis run (with 35, 32, 22 squawks processed) — these are intraday snapshots, not cross-day history.

Use case: comparing today's themes to yesterday's, tracking how narrative shifts over days, morning brief context from the prior session.

Request: ensure history mode returns rows from distinct session_dates. If synthesis runs are hourly, expose a deduplicated view (latest per day) in addition to the raw run-level history.

Context: get_squawk_context(mode='history', date_from='2026-03-10') should return one representative row per day across the week.

value: get_correlation_matrix: expanded from 5 hardcoded pairs to 173 pairs (tuq fix). Full universe working.
get_economic_events countries param: array input fully working as of 2026-03-17. Pass as ['united states'] or as plain string — both work. All econ-data calendar bugs resolved.
econ-data country filter (amb) and mass EU mis-tagging (td2) fixed 2026-03-17 by developer

Beads issue routing: darth-feedor bugs → py_backend-* prefix (/home/dawid/projects/mercury/darth-feedor); market-data bugs → mercury-market-data-* (/home/dawid/projects/mercury/market-data); pubfinance/treasury bugs → treasury-cb-* (/home/dawid/projects/mercury/treasury-cb); econ-data bugs → economic-data-* (/home/dawid/projects/mercury/economic-data). All 4 repos have beads initialized. Cross-cutting issues (rate limiting, health checks) are duplicated into each affected repo. terminalbeta beads is for installer/integration-level issues only, not service-level bugs.
get_economic_history: BLS/BEA historical data tool live. CPI, NFP, GDP, PCE, unemployment back to 2015 (BLS monthly) and 2005 (BEA quarterly). Use list_economic_history_catalog to browse.
get_regime: new cross-asset macro regime tool working. Returns regime label, confidence, drivers, per_complex sub-regimes, and raw features. 5-min cadence.

run_analysis beta test findings (2026-03-18): BUG-1 CRITICAL - all mc.* calls fail with URLError connection refused, sandbox network-isolated from Mercury services (mmd:8001, econ:8010, calendar:5001, treasury:8009). BUG-2 MEDIUM - URL builder omits slash: mc._url('mmd','candles') -> 'http://localhost:8001candles' missing separator, fix in /app/analysis/client.py _url(). BUG-3 MEDIUM - builtins over-restricted, Exception/dir/getattr/hasattr/import all missing, no try/except possible. BUG-4 LOW - mc hardcoded local=True, no API key injected, no way to reach remote API from sandbox. Pure compute engine works fine: math, json, loops, dicts, result return all functional.

econ-data calendar fixed 2026-03-17 (terminalbeta-hof). All follow-on bugs also resolved: country filter (amb/ppr) and mass EU mis-tagging (td2/opw) both fixed 2026-03-17. No open econ-data calendar bugs.
